title: Irreversible migration without backup
description: A destructive schema or data migration is proposed without backup, dry-run, rollback, or owner approval.
body: Failure mode: drop, rename, type narrowing, bulk rewrite, reindex, or delete operations are treated as normal code changes. Detection signals include destructive migration keywords, no expand-contract phase, no data volume estimate, and no rollback plan. Remediation is split into reversible steps and require dry-run and human approval. This blocks automation for production state. Use when a proposed approach skips repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes.
